1.gl: properly, to build up or support; to foster as a parent or nurse; figuratively to render (or be) firm or faithful, to trust or believe, to be permanent or quiet; morally to be true or certain;
1.kj: hence, assurance, believe, bring up, establish, [phrase] fail, be faithful (of long continuance, stedfast, sure, surely, trusty, verified), nurse, (-ing father), (put), trust, turn
1.dv: a primitive root; (Isaiah 30:21; interchangeable with H541 (אָמַן), to go to the right hand)

1.gl: to denude (especially in a disgraceful sense); by implication, to exile (captives being usually stripped); figuratively, to reveal

1.gl: who? (occasionally, by a peculiar idiom, of things); also (indefinitely) whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix
1.kj: any (man), [idiom] he, [idiom] him, [phrase] O that! what, which, who(-m, -se, -soever), [phrase] would to God.
1.dv: an interrogative pronoun of persons, as H4100 (מָה) is of things,

1.gl: the arm (as stretched out), or (of animals) the foreleg; figuratively, force
1.kj: arm, [phrase] help, mighty, power, shoulder, strength.

1.kj: bruit, doctrine, fame, mentioned, news, report, rumor, tidings.

1.gl: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
1.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).
